title: Rainy Season Monsoon Korean Andouille Jjokkang
description: A hearty, cheesy, and savory Korean stew featuring andouille sausage and a variety of vegetables. Serve over a crispy hwajeon pancake for a satisfying and filling meal.
instructions: Heat 1 tablespoon of vegetable oil in a large pot over medium-high heat.
Add the sliced onions and cook until caramelized, about 5 minutes.
Add the minced garlic and cook for 1 minute, until fragrant.
Add the sliced mushrooms and cook until they release their liquid and start to brown, about 5 minutes.
Add the sliced zucchini and cook for 2-3 minutes, until it starts to soften.
Add the gochujang, soy sauce, fish sauce, and brown sugar, and stir to combine.
Add the diced andouille sausage and stir to combine.
Bring the mixture to a simmer and cook for 10-15 minutes, until the flavors have melded together and the sauce has thickened.
Stir in the Korean cheese until melted and well combined.
Serve the stew over a crispy hwajeon pancake and garnish with green onions and toasted sesame seeds, if desired.
Cook the hwajeon pancake by mixing 1 cup of rice flour, 1/2 cup of water, and 1/4 teaspoon of salt in a bowl until a smooth batter forms.
Heat a non-stick skillet over medium heat and pour in the batter to form a small circle.
Cook for 2-3 minutes, until the bottom is crispy and golden brown, then flip and cook for an additional 1-2 minutes, until the other side is also crispy and golden brown.
